behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate would inject a synthetic nested-path event into the watcher adapter and assert pipeline invocation; it catches path-filter bugs in app code. System candidate creates a real subdirectory/file under a temp watched root and waits for real watcher events; it catches misconfigured recursive watching and OS-specific watcher semantics. Lower level miss: a fake event bypasses actual subscription behavior, so recursive-watch failures stay invisible. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires real watch coverage of files "inside a subdirectory of the watched root," which is watcher/OS event behavior, not only in-process logic.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against a real temp directory tree, create `root/sub/a.txt`, assert exactly one transformed output appears for that file path within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate tests dedup map/window math in isolation with a fake clock; it catches TTL arithmetic bugs. Integration candidate wires intake->dedup->scheduler and submits the same path twice within the window; it catches missed dedup hook-up or wrong call ordering. Lower level miss: pure unit cannot prove the daemon actually consults dedup before scheduling processing. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI/ML dependency; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: In-process test with real daemon modules (stub transform/output), fake clock or controllable timers, enqueue same path twice within configured window, assert one processing execution and one discard metric/log.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate emits duplicate synthetic events and asserts one process call; it catches dedup-by-event-key logic bugs. System candidate performs a real write pattern known to trigger multiple FS events and verifies single processing; it catches watcher-library/OS event burst behavior. Lower level miss: mocked emitters cannot reproduce real kernel/event-coalescing patterns reliably. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly depends on "operating system emits multiple filesystem events."
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon on real temp directory, perform single file write/rename sequence that triggers multiple native events, assert exactly one output artifact and one pipeline run for that path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate simulates watcher "unlinkDir/addDir" callbacks and asserts re-subscribe code path; it catches state-machine bugs. System candidate deletes and recreates the actual watched directory and then writes a new file; it catches inode/path rebind and real watcher reattachment failures. Lower level miss: synthetic callbacks do not validate that the underlying watcher actually reattaches after real directory recreation. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires handling directory removal/recreation "while the daemon is running," an OS-level filesystem lifecycle condition.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon with real watched directory, remove directory, recreate same path, place new file, assert daemon processes it without restart and continues watching.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate injects pending-file records with controlled timestamps and checks queue ordering; it catches sort/comparator bugs. System candidate creates multiple real files nearly simultaneously and verifies observed processing order against filesystem timestamps; it catches timestamp acquisition granularity/tie and event-order interaction issues. Lower level miss: injected records bypass real file metadata reads and race timing between watcher events and stat calls. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text ties correctness to actual file "creation timestamps" under simultaneous arrival, which depends on real FS metadata and event timing.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on temp dir, create N files concurrently, record their filesystem creation/birth timestamps as seen by daemon path-stat step, assert processing sequence follows required timestamp ordering (with explicit tie-break rule if defined).

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate stubs file-ready checks and simulates partial/complete states; it catches readiness-policy logic bugs. System candidate performs a slow chunked real write so arrival event precedes completion, then verifies processing starts only after final close/settle; it catches real FS visibility/locking/timing behavior. Lower level miss: stubs cannot reproduce actual write-in-progress semantics or event timing races. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly depends on real-time write completion state when "arrival event fires."
behaviors.test_description: Create file via slow stream/chunked writer in watched dir, assert daemon does not invoke transform until writer closes and file size/content stabilizes per configured completion policy.
